questions.question: Which ocean is to the south of India?
questions.explanation: India is bounded by the Indian Ocean to the south, the Bay of Bengal to the southeast, and the Arabian Sea to the southwest.

questions.question: The Palk Strait separates India from:
questions.explanation: The Palk Strait separates Tamil Nadu in India from the Northern Province of Sri Lanka.

questions.question: The Palk Strait separates:
options: India and Sri Lanka (between Tamil Nadu and Sri Lanka)
questions.explanation: The Palk Strait separates India (Tamil Nadu) from Sri Lanka. It is about 64–137 km wide. The Gulf of Mannar lies to the south and Palk Bay to the north of the strait.

questions.question: The Suez Canal connects:
questions.explanation: The Suez Canal (193 km, Egypt) connects the Red Sea to the Mediterranean Sea. Opened in 1869, it allows ships to travel between Europe and Asia without going around Africa, reducing the journey by ~7,000 km.

questions.question: The Panama Canal connects:
options: Gulf of Mexico and Caribbean Sea
questions.explanation: The Panama Canal (82 km, Panama) connects the Atlantic (via Caribbean Sea) and Pacific Ocean. Opened in 1914, ships avoid going around South America (Cape Horn), saving about 12,000 km.

questions.question: The Pamban Island (Rameswaram) is connected to mainland by:
options: Pamban Bridge (railway) and Annai Indira Gandhi Road Bridge
questions.explanation: Pamban (Rameswaram) island is connected by Pamban Railway Bridge (India's first sea bridge, 1914) and Annai Indira Gandhi Road Bridge (1988). The new cable-stayed rail bridge is under construction.

questions.question: India's first Metro rail was established in:
questions.explanation: Kolkata Metro was India's first metro rail system, inaugurated on 24 October 1984 (though partial opening was in 1983). Delhi Metro (2002) is currently the largest metro network in India.

questions.question: The Strait of Gibraltar separates:
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar (14 km wide at its narrowest) separates Spain (Europe) from Morocco (Africa). It connects the Atlantic Ocean to the Mediterranean Sea. Gibraltar is a British Overseas Territory.

questions.question: The English Channel separates:
questions.explanation: The English Channel (La Manche in French) separates southern England from northern France. The Channel Tunnel (Chunnel, 1994) connects the two countries under the sea. The strait is 33 km wide at its narrowest (Dover-Calais).

questions.question: The Strait of Malacca connects:
options: Indian Ocean and South China Sea (between Malaysia and Indonesia)
Bay of Bengal and South China Sea
questions.explanation: The Strait of Malacca (between Malaysia/Singapore and Sumatra/Indonesia) is one of the world's most important shipping lanes, connecting the Indian Ocean with the South China Sea and Pacific Ocean.

questions.question: The Bering Strait separates:
questions.explanation: The Bering Strait (85 km wide) separates Russia (Siberia, Asia) from the USA (Alaska, North America), connecting the Pacific Ocean (Bering Sea) with the Arctic Ocean. A proposed land bridge existed here during ice ages.

questions.question: Which is India's longest road tunnel?
options: Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee Tunnel (Banihal-Qazigund, J&K)
questions.explanation: Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee Tunnel (Banihal-Qazigund Tunnel, 8.45 km rail tunnel) is part of the J&K railway. The Atal (Rohtang) Road Tunnel (9.02 km) is currently India's longest road tunnel.

questions.question: The Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park (Tamil Nadu) is famous for:
options: Coral reefs, dugongs, sea turtles, and diverse marine life
questions.explanation: Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park (21 islands between Rameswaram and Tuticorin) is the first marine national park in South and Southeast Asia. Famous for coral reefs, dugongs, sea turtles, sea cucumbers, and 3,600+ species.

questions.question: The 'Dokar La' Pass and 'Jelep La' Pass are on the border of:
questions.explanation: Jelep La Pass (4,270 m) is on the Sikkim-China (Tibet) border and was a major trade route before 1962. Nathu La (4,310 m) is the currently open border crossing between Sikkim and Tibet.

questions.question: The International Date Line roughly follows:
options: The 180° meridian (with deviations for island nations)
questions.explanation: The International Date Line (IDL) roughly follows the 180° meridian in the Pacific Ocean, but deviates to avoid splitting countries. Going west across the IDL, the date advances by one day. Going east, the date goes back by one day.

questions.question: The deepest ocean trench in the world is:
questions.explanation: The Mariana Trench in the western Pacific Ocean is the world's deepest point at ~11,034m (Challenger Deep). James Cameron reached the bottom in 2012 in a submersible.

questions.question: The International Date Line runs approximately along:
questions.explanation: The International Date Line runs approximately along the 180° meridian in the Pacific Ocean, deviating to avoid splitting countries. Crossing it westward gains a day; eastward loses a day.

questions.question: The Palk Bay lies between:
options: Tamil Nadu coast and the Palk Strait to the east
India and Sri Lanka on all sides
questions.explanation: The Palk Bay is a shallow bay bounded by Tamil Nadu on the west, the Palk Strait on the east (between India and Sri Lanka), and Rameshwaram/Pamban Island on the south. Fishing is important here.

questions.question: What is the largest ocean that borders the eastern coast of India?
questions.explanation: The Indian Ocean is the largest ocean bordering the eastern coast of India. It plays a significant role in India's climate and trade.
